            # Blueprint-writer directive — chapter `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` (iter-227)

You edit ONLY `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`. Three precise,
additive edits. Do NOT touch any other block, do NOT add/remove `\leanok` or `\mathlibok`
(the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase owns `\leanok`).

## Strategy context (the slice that matters)

This chapter blueprints the by-hand commutative-group law on iso-classes of locally-trivial
(invertible) `O_X`-modules under tensor product (the substrate for the relative Picard sheaf
`Pic_{C/k}`). The remaining mathematical work is the "descent re-route": the ⊗-inverse
(`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`, `exists_tensorObj_inverse`) and the associator
(`tensorObj_assoc_iso`) are built by **gluing canonical local trivialising isomorphisms** (obtained
from the already-closed `tensorObj_restrict_iso` + `tensorObj_unit_iso`) into a global morphism of
sheaves of modules, then concluding it is a global iso because "being an isomorphism is local."
This route was confirmed by a Mathlib-idiom consult (analogist ts226descent) to genuinely AVOID the
abandoned tensor-stalk commutation ("d.2"); every bridge rests on an existing Mathlib primitive.

Two of the three descent bridges are now realised in Lean but are NOT yet named blocks in this
chapter (they appear only as inline phrases inside `rem:dual_discharges_inverse` and the proof of
`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`). A per-file blueprint check flagged this: the bridges need named,
trackable lemma blocks so `sync_leanok` can pin them and the prover has an explicit target. All three
edits below are project-bespoke category-theory infrastructure built on cited Mathlib primitives —
treat them as **Archon-original blocks** (no external `% SOURCE:`/`% SOURCE QUOTE:` lines required;
the blocks stand on their own statement + sketch, naming the Mathlib primitives in prose). If while
drafting you decide a Stacks "gluing morphisms of sheaves" tag (e.g. the sheaf-Hom/descent section)
would strengthen a citation, you MAY dispatch a reference-retriever (your write-domain includes
`references/**`) and quote it verbatim — but this is optional; these are infrastructure lemmas.

## Edit 1 — add the `archon:covers` annotation

Near the very top of the file, just after `\label{chap:Picard_TensorObjSubstrate}` (mirroring how
`Picard_QuotScheme.tex` does it on its line 3), add the line:

    % archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean

(This declares the chapter as the consolidated blueprint for that Lean file, which it already is in
practice.)

## Edit 2 — named block for the "locally-iso ⇒ global iso" B-connector

Add a new `\begin{lemma} ... \end{lemma}` + `\begin{proof} ... \end{proof}` block inside the dual
infrastructure section `\label{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}` (place it just BEFORE
`rem:dual_discharges_inverse`, i.e. before line ~2775, since that remark already invokes "being an
isomorphism is local"). Specifications:

- `\label{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}`
- `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_restrict}`
- `\uses{}` — none required (it is a standalone locality principle); you may leave `\uses{}` absent.
- Human title: `[Local isomorphisms of O_X-modules glue to a global isomorphism]`.
- Statement (project notation): Let `X` be a scheme and `phi : M --> N` a morphism in `X.Modules`
  (= `SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf`). Suppose given, for every point `x` of `X`, an open
  neighbourhood `U x` of `x` such that the restriction of `phi` to `U x` (i.e.
  `(Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).i).map phi`) is an isomorphism. Then `phi` is an
  isomorphism.
- Proof sketch (the route that was formalised, stalkwise): "being an isomorphism" of a morphism of
  sheaves of `O_X`-modules can be checked on stalks. For each point `x`, choose a preimage `x'` of
  `x` in `U x` under the open immersion `(U x).i`. The restriction of `phi` to `U x` is an
  isomorphism by hypothesis, hence so is its image under any functor; transporting along the natural
  isomorphism "restriction commutes with stalks" (`restrictStalkNatIso`) shows the stalk of `phi` at
  `x` (as a morphism of the underlying sheaves of abelian groups) is an isomorphism. As this holds at
  every point and a morphism of sheaves of abelian groups with isomorphism stalks is an isomorphism
  (`isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` on `TopCat.Sheaf`), the underlying ab-sheaf morphism is an iso;
  the forgetful functor `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` reflects isomorphisms, so `phi` itself is an iso.
  Name in prose the Mathlib pieces used: stalkwise iso-detection for sheaves, the
  restriction-commutes-with-stalks natural iso, and reflection of isos along the forgetful functor.

## Edit 3 — named block for the A-bridge (SheafOfModules morphism descent)

Add a second new `\begin{lemma} ... \end{lemma}` + `\begin{proof}` block, also inside
`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra` (place it right AFTER the B-connector block of Edit 2, before
`rem:dual_discharges_inverse`). This is the genuinely-novel infrastructure step the prover will build
next, so the sketch must be detailed enough to formalise. Specifications:

- `\label{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}`
- `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat}`  (a forward pin; the Lean declaration
  does not exist yet — that is expected and correct for a not-yet-formalised block.)
- `\uses{}` — none strictly required; you may reference `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso` if you cite it
  in the sketch, otherwise leave absent.
- Human title: `[Compatible local morphisms of O_X-modules glue to a global morphism]`.
- Statement (project notation): Let `X` be a scheme, `M N : X.Modules`, and `{U_i}` an open cover of
  `X`. Suppose given for each `i` a morphism `f_i : M|_{U_i} --> N|_{U_i}` such that for all `i, j`
  the restrictions of `f_i` and `f_j` to `U_i ∩ U_j` agree. Then there is a (unique) global morphism
  `f : M --> N` in `X.Modules` whose restriction to each `U_i` is `f_i`.
- Proof sketch (the intended formalisation route): A morphism of sheaves of `O_X`-modules is, in
  particular, a morphism of the underlying sheaves of abelian groups that is additionally
  `O_X`-linear sectionwise. (i) GLUE THE UNDERLYING AB-SHEAF MORPHISM. Forget `M, N` to their
  underlying sheaves of abelian groups via the faithful additive functor
  `SheafOfModules.toSheaf`. The hom-presheaf `U \mapsto Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` of sheaves of abelian groups
  is itself a sheaf (the morphism-gluing / "hom into a sheaf is a sheaf" principle,
  `Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom` / `sheafHomSectionsEquiv` in `CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom`). Hence the
  compatible family `(f_i)` — being a compatible family of sections of this hom-sheaf over the cover
  — amalgamates to a unique global section, i.e. a global morphism `g` of the underlying ab-sheaves
  restricting to each `f_i`. (ii) PROMOTE TO `O_X`-LINEAR. The `O_X`-linearity equation
  `g(r · m) = r · g(m)` is a sectionwise identity; it holds on each `U_i` (because `f_i` is a module
  morphism) and the two sides are sections of a separated presheaf agreeing on a cover, so it holds
  globally. Package `g` together with this global linearity as a morphism `f : M --> N` in
  `X.Modules` (`PresheafOfModules.homMk`). Uniqueness and the restriction property are inherited from
  the ab-sheaf amalgamation. Name in prose the Mathlib primitives: the hom-sheaf/descent statement in
  `CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom`, the faithful additive `SheafOfModules.toSheaf`, and
  `PresheafOfModules.homMk`. Note explicitly (one sentence) that this descent step computes NO tensor
  stalk — it is a statement about morphisms of sheaves — and so does not re-enter the abandoned
  tensor-stalk commutation.

## Out of scope (do NOT do)
- Do NOT edit `rem:dual_discharges_inverse`, `lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial`,

1	\chapter{Relative Picard sheaf --- \texttt{Scheme.Modules.tensorObj} substrate (A.1.c.SubT)}
2	\label{chap:Picard_TensorObjSubstrate}
3	
4	\section{Motivation: the abelian-group-valued relative Picard sheaf}
5	\label{sec:tensorobj_motivation}
6	
7	Fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C\) over
8	\(k\). In Kleiman's framework (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor},
9	\cref{def:rel_pic_sharp}), the relative Picard functor
10	\[
11	  \Pic_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat,
12	  \qquad
13	  T \;\longmapsto\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^* \Pic(T),
14	\]
15	is, by definition, abelian-group-valued. The abelian-group structure on each

2720	  each open \(W \subseteq U\) and section \(s \in M(W)\), a well-defined section of
2721	  \(N(W)\) by gluing the \(\varphi_i(s|_{W \cap U_i})\) (these agree on overlaps by
2722	  hypothesis, and \(N\) is a sheaf so the glued section is unique). The resulting
2723	  assignment is \(R(W)\)-linear and natural in \(W\), hence a morphism
2724	  \(M|_U \to N|_U\) restricting to the \(\varphi_i\); uniqueness is the separatedness
2725	  of \(N\). This is exactly the sheaf condition for \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\), so it is a
2726	  sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Taking \(N = \mathcal{O}_X\) yields
2727	  \(\mathtt{dual}\,M\), and the evaluation morphism of \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval},
2728	  being a morphism of presheaves into the sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\), is a morphism of
2729	  the corresponding sheaves of modules.
2730	\end{proof}
2731	
2732	\begin{lemma}
2733	[The dual of a line bundle is a line bundle]
2734	  \label{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}
2735	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.dual_isLocallyTrivial}
2736	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
2737	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 01CR (Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Invertible
2738	  % modules), Lemma~lemma-constructions-invertible (item~2)
2739	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4200--L4213).
2740	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2741	  % "\item If $\mathcal{L}$ is an invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module, then so is
2742	  % $\SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X)$ and the evaluation map
2743	  % $\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X}
2744	  % \SheafHom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X$
2745	  % is an isomorphism."
2746	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CR,
2747	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-invertible} (item~2).}
2748	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(L \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with
2749	  \(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,L\). Then the sheaf-level dual
2750	  \(\mathtt{dual}\,L\) of \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} is again locally trivial of
2751	  rank one, i.e.\ \(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,L)\).
2752	\end{lemma}
2753	
2754	\begin{proof}
2755	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
2756	  Local triviality of \(L\) furnishes, around each point, an open \(U\) with a
2757	  trivialisation \(L|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\). The internal hom commutes with
2758	  open-immersion restriction --- the slice formula
2759	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom} is defined through \(L|_U\), so
2760	  \((\mathtt{dual}\,L)|_U \cong \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(L|_U, \mathcal{O}_U)\),
2761	  the restriction-compatibility being the dual analogue of
2762	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} for the internal hom. Under the trivialisation,
2763	  \[
2764	    (\mathtt{dual}\,L)|_U
2765	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{O}_U)
2766	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_U,
2767	  \]
2768	  the last identification being evaluation at \(1\), the canonical iso of the dual of
2769	  a free rank-one module with itself. Hence \(\mathtt{dual}\,L\) is locally trivial of
2770	  rank one. (Equivalently, this is item~2 of Stacks
2771	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-invertible}, tag 01CR: the dual of an invertible
2772	  module is invertible.)
2773	\end{proof}
2774	
2775	\begin{remark}
2776	[How the dual infrastructure discharges the \(\otimes\)-inverse]
2777	  \label{rem:dual_discharges_inverse}
2778	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial,
2779	        lem:internal_hom_eval, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
2780	  Once \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom,def:presheaf_dual,lem:internal_hom_eval,%
2781	  lem:internal_hom_isSheaf,lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial} are available,
2782	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} is discharged with no further missing
2783	  object: take \(L^{-1} := \mathtt{dual}\,L\) (\cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf}), which
2784	  is a line bundle by \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}, and take \(\varepsilon_L\) to
2785	  be the descended evaluation of \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}. On a trivialising open
2786	  \(U\), the restriction-compatibility iso \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} carries
2787	  \((L \otimes_X L^{-1})|_U\) to \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U\) and
2788	  \(\varepsilon_L|_U\) to the contraction \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U \to
2789	  \mathcal{O}_U\) of the free rank-one module with its dual, which is the left unitor
2790	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} and hence an isomorphism. As ``being an isomorphism''
2791	  is local on \(X\), the global \(\varepsilon_L\) is an isomorphism
2792	  \(L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\) by
2793	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} together with the structure-sheaf-unit comparison
2794	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}. This is the realization the
2795	  ``infrastructure-blocked'' note in \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} points
2796	  to.
2797	\end{remark}
2798	
2799	\begin{remark}
2800	[Alternative route via object-level descent (fallback)]
2801	  \label{rem:dual_via_stack}
2802	  Should the slice-and-sheafify route of
2803	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom,lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} bottom out, the dual can
2804	  instead be assembled by \emph{object-level descent}: glue the local trivial duals
2805	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) along the inverse-transpose transition isomorphisms
2806	  \(g_{ij}^{-\mathsf{T}}\) of \(L\) into a global \(L^{-1}\) on \(X\). Abstractly this
2807	  is effective descent for sheaves of modules on the small Zariski site --- the
2808	  essential-surjectivity of the descent-data functor of an \(\mathtt{IsStack}\)
2809	  pseudofunctor \(U \mapsto (\mathcal{O}_U\text{-modules})\). This is the more

1430:  the \(\otimes\)-inverse needs (\cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}); accordingly, the
1628:  \cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra} (\cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom} through
1685:  \cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}}, every line bundle \(L\) has the two-sided tensor
1691:  and its evaluation of \cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}.

input.new_string:
\begin{lemma}
[Local isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules glue to a global isomorphism]
  \label{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_restrict}
  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi \colon M \to N\) a morphism in
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\).
  Suppose that for every point \(x \in X\) there is an open neighbourhood \(U_x\) of
  \(x\) such that the restriction of \(\varphi\) to \(U_x\) --- the image
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(U_x).\mathtt{i}).\mathtt{map}\,\varphi\) under the
  restriction functor along the open immersion \((U_x).\mathtt{i}\colon U_x \to X\) ---
  is an isomorphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U_x\). Then \(\varphi\) is an
  isomorphism.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  Being an isomorphism for a morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules can be
  checked on the underlying morphism of sheaves of abelian groups, and there it can be
  checked stalkwise. Fix a point \(x \in X\). Choose a preimage \(x' \in U_x\) of \(x\)
  under the open immersion \((U_x).\mathtt{i}\). By hypothesis the restriction
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(U_x).\mathtt{i}).\mathtt{map}\,\varphi\) is an
  isomorphism, hence so is its image under any functor --- in particular under the
  stalk-at-\(x'\) functor on the underlying ab-sheaves. The natural isomorphism
  ``restriction commutes with stalks'' (\(\mathtt{restrictStalkNatIso}\)) identifies
  this with the stalk of (the underlying ab-sheaf morphism of) \(\varphi\) at the image
  point \(x\); transporting along it shows that stalk is an isomorphism. As this holds
  at every \(x\), and a morphism of sheaves of abelian groups all of whose stalks are
  isomorphisms is itself an isomorphism (stalkwise iso-detection,
  \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_stalkFunctor\_map\_iso}\) on \(\mathtt{TopCat.Sheaf}\)), the
  underlying ab-sheaf morphism of \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism. Finally the forgetful
  functor \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules.toPresheaf}\) reflects isomorphisms, so \(\varphi\)
  itself is an isomorphism.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Compatible local morphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules glue to a global morphism]
  \label{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat}
  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and \(\{U_i\}\) an
  open cover of \(X\). Suppose given, for each \(i\), a morphism
  \(f_i \colon M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U_i\) such that
  for all \(i, j\) the restrictions of \(f_i\) and \(f_j\) to \(U_i \cap U_j\) agree.
  Then there is a unique global morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) whose restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  A morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is, concretely, a morphism of the
  underlying sheaves of abelian groups that is in addition \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear
  sectionwise. We build \(f\) in two steps.

  \emph{(i) Glue the underlying ab-sheaf morphism.} Forget \(M\) and \(N\) to their
  underlying sheaves of abelian groups via the faithful additive functor
  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.toSheaf}\). The assignment
  \(U \mapsto \mathrm{Hom}(M|_U, N|_U)\) on the underlying ab-sheaves is itself a sheaf
  --- this is the morphism-gluing / ``hom into a sheaf is a sheaf'' principle
  (\(\mathtt{Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom}\) / \(\mathtt{sheafHomSectionsEquiv}\) in
  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom}\)). The given family \((f_i)\) is, after
  forgetting to ab-sheaves, a compatible family of sections of this hom-sheaf over the
  cover \(\{U_i\}\) (compatibility is exactly the agreement on the overlaps
  \(U_i \cap U_j\)). By the sheaf condition it amalgamates to a unique global section,
  i.e.\ a global morphism \(g\) of the underlying ab-sheaves whose restriction to each
  \(U_i\) is the ab-sheaf morphism underlying \(f_i\).

  \emph{(ii) Promote to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear.} The linearity equation
  \(g(r \cdot m) = r \cdot g(m)\) is a sectionwise identity between two sections of a
  separated presheaf. It holds on each \(U_i\), because there \(g\) restricts to the
  module morphism \(f_i\); since the two sides agree on the cover \(\{U_i\}\) and the
  ambient presheaf is separated, they agree globally. Packaging \(g\) together with this
  global linearity yields a morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) (via \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.homMk}\)). Its
  restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\) and its uniqueness are both inherited from the
  ab-sheaf amalgamation of step (i).

  This descent step computes no tensor stalk --- it is purely a statement about gluing
  morphisms of sheaves --- and so it does not re-enter the abandoned tensor-stalk
  commutation.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}
[How the dual infrastructure discharges the \(\otimes\)-inverse]
  \label{rem:dual_discharges_inverse}

## Summary

I made three precise additive edits to `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` (and only that file):

**Edit 1** — Added `% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` at line 3, right after the chapter label.

**Edit 2** — New lemma block `lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict` pinned to `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_restrict` (the already-closed B-connector). It states the locality principle (per-point local iso ⇒ global iso) and the proof sketch names the stalkwise route: `isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso`, `restrictStalkNatIso`, and reflection along `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf`.

**Edit 3** — New lemma block `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` with a **forward pin** to `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat` (not-yet-formalised, the prover's next target). It states compatible-local-morphisms-glue and gives the detailed two-step formalisation route (glue ab-sheaf morphism via `Sites.SheafHom`'s `Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom`/`sheafHomSectionsEquiv` through `SheafOfModules.toSheaf`, then promote to O_X-linear via separatedness + `PresheafOfModules.homMk`), with the explicit "computes no tensor stalk" disclaimer.

Both new blocks sit inside `sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`, in order, immediately before `rem:dual_discharges_inverse`. No `\leanok`/`\mathlibok` added, no existing block altered, and no reference-retriever dispatched (it was optional for these infrastructure blocks).
